001package edu.cnm.deepdive.pippiphooray.controller;
002
003import android.os.Bundle;
004import android.view.LayoutInflater;
005import android.view.View;
006import android.view.ViewGroup;
007import androidx.annotation.NonNull;
008import androidx.annotation.Nullable;
009import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment;
010import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModelProvider;
011import androidx.navigation.Navigation;
012import dagger.hilt.android.AndroidEntryPoint;
013import edu.cnm.deepdive.pippiphooray.R;
014import edu.cnm.deepdive.pippiphooray.databinding.FragmentSignInBinding;
015import edu.cnm.deepdive.pippiphooray.viewmodel.SignInViewModel;
016
017/**
018 * Fragment that handles user authentication with Google Sign-In.
019 *
020 * <p>This screen initiates interactive sign-in, observes authentication state,
021 * and attempts silent sign-in when possible.
022 */
023@AndroidEntryPoint
024public class SignInFragment extends Fragment {
025
026  private FragmentSignInBinding binding;
027  private SignInViewModel signInViewModel;
028
029  /**
030   * Inflates the sign-in layout and configures the sign-in button.
031   *
032   * @param inflater layout inflater.
033   * @param container optional parent container.
034   * @param savedInstanceState saved instance state, if any.
035   * @return root view for this fragment.
036   */
037  @Nullable
038  @Override
039  public View onCreateView(
040      @NonNull LayoutInflater inflater,
041      @Nullable ViewGroup container,
042      @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState
043  ) {
044    binding = FragmentSignInBinding.inflate(inflater, container, false);
045    binding.signInButton.setOnClickListener(
046        (v) -> {
047          binding.signInButton.setEnabled(false);
048          signInViewModel.signIn(requireActivity());
049        });
050    return binding.getRoot();
051  }
052
053  /**
054   * Completes fragment setup by connecting the UI to the sign-in ViewModel.
055   *
056   * @param view root view returned from {@link #onCreateView}.
057   * @param savedInstanceState saved instance state, if any.
058   */
059  @Override
060  public void onViewCreated(@NonNull View view, @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState) {
061    super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState);
062    signInViewModel = new ViewModelProvider(requireActivity())
063        .get(SignInViewModel.class);
064
065    signInViewModel
066        .getCredential()
067        .observe(getViewLifecycleOwner(), (credential) -> {
068          if (credential != null) {
069            Navigation.findNavController(binding.getRoot())
070                .navigate(R.id.navigate_to_batch_list_fragment);
071          } else {
072            binding.signInButton.setEnabled(true);
073          }
074        });
075
076    signInViewModel
077        .getThrowable()
078        .observe(getViewLifecycleOwner(), (throwable) -> {
079          if (throwable != null) {
080            binding.signInButton.setEnabled(true);
081            binding.signInButton.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE);
082            // TODO: show a Snackbar or error text here.
083          }
084        });
085
086    // Attempt silent sign-in similar to capstone's signInQuickly.
087    signInViewModel.signInQuickly(requireActivity());
088  }
089
090  /**
091   * Clears the view binding reference when the fragment view is destroyed.
092   */
093  @Override
094  public void onDestroyView() {
095    binding = null;
096    super.onDestroyView();
097  }
098
099}
